title: Aconitella scleroclada

side_effects:
Aconitella scleroclada contains aconitine and other compounds from the aconitum family, which can be toxic to humans and animals if ingested or improperly handled.

Aconitella scleroclada's toxic compounds can cause a range of side effects, including nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.

In severe cases, the plant's toxins can lead to neurological symptoms such as numbness, weakness, and even paralysis.

The plant's toxic compounds can also interact with other medications and exacerbate pre-existing health conditions.

If ingested, Aconitella scleroclada can be fatal if not treated promptly.
intro: Aconitella scleroclada, also known as Aconitella, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate pain and reduce the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Aconitella scleroclada include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Aconitella is often used in preparations such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments, although its use should be carefully monitored due to its potential toxicity.
